observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Draw Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the overstatement of Loans Receivable – Others and the understatement to Donations Account as follows:



Henceforth, strictly adhere with the prescribed revised chart of accounts for LGUs per COA Circular 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that said issue was discussed during a meeting of all Municipal Accountants of Ilocos Sur, which was also attended by COA Audit Team Leaders. However, due to differing interpretations and the absence of a clear consensus or guidance on the matter, no final resolution was reached. As a result, the audit recommendation was not implemented pending further clarification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the optimal utilization of the RA No. 7171 Municipal Share through the proper and timely implementation of programs and projects so that the benefits expected from these programs and projects will be expeditiously delivered to the tobacco farmers. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to improve project scheduling to prevent delays in the completion and enable the Municipality to take the appropriate action in case slippages occur.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to implement all the PPA cited in AAR 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
The Municipal Treasurer to:
send a written notice to the payees to inform them of the checks that have become stale;
issue new checks to payees who responded to the notice and upon submission of the stale checks; and
henceforth adhere to the provision of Section 59 of NGAS Volume I to send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale.
The Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting entries to properly record the cancellation and replacement of stale checks in accordance with Section 59 of NGAS Volume I.

We recommended Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to intensify efforts in pursuing collection by following up with debtors, issuing the appropriate demand letters, and conducting a thorough review of the aforementioned receivables to substantiate dormant receivables in support of the Municipality’s claim. Additionally, the Municipal Accountant should assess the collectability of long-outstanding receivables and recognize provisions for any deemed uncollectible amounts, as necessary. Should there be no other remedies for collection, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive request authority to write-off dormant receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008, and other relevant COA Circulars regarding writing off of receivables, ensuring that all required documentation is attached to avoid further delays.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, MPDC and other officials concerned, ensure the full utilization of the 20% DF, which significantly contribute to achieving desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the municipality and its intended beneficiaries. Concerned officials shall likewise look into the root cause of these delays and immediately initiate measures to address the problem promptly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and MPDC, ensure the optimal utilization of the RA 7171 Municipal Share through the proper project planning and timely implementation of programs and projects so that the benefits expected from these programs and projects will be expeditiously delivered to the tobacco farmers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned offices to comply with Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 by submitting to the Auditor, at the beginning of each year, a comprehensive list of all ongoing and planned Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs). The list should include the required details such as project name, implementing office, description, contractor or supplier (if any), mode of procurement, funding source, approved budget, project duration, and location. Timely submission of this list will enable the Audit Team to conduct effective validation and monitoring, and allow for the early detection and communication of any deficiencies for appropriate and immediate management action.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) refrain from participating in any BAC function, including the approval of BAC Resolutions related to bid evaluation and post-qualification. The HOPE should act only upon the final recommendation of award, after the BAC has completed its mandate in the bidding process. Future procurement activities must strictly adhere to the delineated roles under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR to promote transparency, accountability, and legality in the procurement process.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure all future Certificates of Acceptance are properly dated, and that a separate Certificate of Final Acceptance is issued only after the one-year Defects Liability Period has elapsed. Performance Securities may state that they remain valid “until the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance,” provided that the certificate is properly dated and clearly issued after the Defects Liability Period. These actions will help ensure compliance with procurement rules and protect the Municipality’s right to enforce contractor warranties and require defect repairs when necessary.

observations.managementAction: Complied. The recommended adjusting journal entry was prepared and recorded by the Municipal Accountant, thereby correcting the misstatements in the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Boards and Awards Committee to discontinue purchases requested by offices that has reference to brand names in order to comply with Section 18 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Likewise, judiciously assess the validity and legality of submitted purchase requests and its supporting documents before proceeding with the purchase.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) has instructed the BAC Secretariat to ensure that all items included in procurement documents do not make reference to any specific brand name, in adherence to procurement regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC to post on the PhilGEPS website all awards within the prescribed period of three calendar days to manifest and attain transparency in the procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) advised the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the posting of the Notice of Award (NOA) on the PhilGEPS website is done within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC to properly accomplish and review the completeness of the documents submitted, especially the date, to determine if the procurement was within the time frame provided by Annex “C” as prescribed in Section 38 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No.  9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to fully implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality consider the advantages of engaging in a Framework Agreement facilitated through Competitive Bidding. This approach will ensure efficiency in procurement, minimize delays in acquisition, provide immediate delivery on short notice, and offer fixed or adjusted prices for the duration of the agreement, among other benefits. Furthermore, by exploring the utilization of Framework Agreements in accordance with GPPB Resolution No. 27-2019, the Municipality can enhance transparency, fairness, and value for money in its procurement processes while effectively meeting the needs of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management attempted to utilize the Framework Agreement as the mode of procurement for the purchase of meals and snacks, but resulted to failed bidding; thus, they had to revert back to the SVP as the mode of procurement for such purchases.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:

All  offices, when requesting payments for DVs, to submit/attach all documents necessary to validate claims for transactions;



The Municipal Accountant to meticulously review all attachments submitted to the accounting offices before certifying the completeness of supporting documents in the DV; and

The Bids and Awards Committee to require suppliers to include additional necessary documents for unique procurements such as purchase of chemicals.
observations.managementAction:
Management has fully complied with the audit recommendations. All offices have been directed to ensure that Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) are submitted with complete supporting documents to validate claims.

observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee complied with the recommendation. Notification letters were being sent to losing bidders through mail and email, verified after review and evaluation of pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the MSWMB complete the approval process of its 10-year SWM Plan in compliance with Section 16 of RA 9003 in order that protection of public health and environment will be ensured.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The Management consistently followed–up the status of their 10- year SWM Plan at EMB and were informed that the deliberation for the approved of their SWM Plan was set on the last week of April, 2022.
observations.reason: No copy of the 10- year SWM Plan was shown to the team.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and monitoring of receivables, we recommended that the Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to send collection/demand letters (at least three times) to the loan borrowers and ensure the same are duly received by the latter. If collection is nil, receiving copies of demand/collection letters will be used to support the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables.
b. The Local Chief Executive may file request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts without any possibility of collection in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to present fairly the receivable account in the financial statements.
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and analyze on a regular basis all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The Management requested for the write-off of the accounts receivables.
observations.reason: Received the request for write-off, however evaluation of the request showed that some of the documentary requirements were absent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record disbursements of trust receipts under the Trust Fund in accordance with the provisions of COA Memo 2020-008 dated March 11, 2020 so as not to understate both assets and government equity of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with each other for the immediate remittances of the Real Property Tax share to the Provincial Government in accordance with Section 293 of RA 7160 thus, the later would have additional funds for the implementation of its priority projects.

We recommended that Management require the BAC and/or GSO to submit to the Auditor copies of perfected contracts and their supporting documents within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order for the later to review and evaluate the same, thus any deficiency could be communicated to Management for immediate and appropriate action.

We recommended that Management instruct the Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Officer to include in their Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Plan programs/projects/activities related to the prevention, mitigation and response to disasters provided in the National Risk Reduction Management Council, Department of Budget and Management and Department of Interior and Local Government Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, thus the purpose for which the fund was created would be achieved.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to coordinate with each other and ensure the full implementation of programs/projects/activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the municipality and its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that that Management require the Bid and Awards Committee to invite observers for their procurements made thru public bidding in accordance with those enumerated in Section 13.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to enhance transparency in the procurement process.
We recommended that the Management settle their audit suspension before the 90 calendar days period to avoid suspensions maturing into disallowances.

We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor a copy of the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year in order for the audit team to verify the existence of the PPE and to ascertain the accounts presented in the financial statements, thus any discrepancies would be immediately communicated to management for its prompt and appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The General Service Officer (GSO) submitted the RPCPPE to the Auditor.
observations.reason: Received the RPCPPE from the General Service Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to demand official receipts from the payees/creditors in acknowledging the payment/remittance made by the municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 2004-006 dated September 9, 2004 and in order that the reliability, validity, truthfulness of the payment/remittance made could be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The Municipal Treasurer submitted the official receipts to the Auditor.
observations.reason: Received the official receipts and settled the corresponding Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the GAD focal person furnish the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report as provided for in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 in order to verify on whether gender issues contained in the GAD Plan were fully addressed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The GAD Focal Person submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report to the team.
observations.reason: Received the GAD Accomplishment Report submitted by the Gad Focal Person.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management settle their audit suspension before the 90 calendar days period to avoid suspensions maturing into disallowances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The Management settled their suspensions in the amount of ₱2,750,894.07.
observations.reason: Issued the NSSDC on the suspensions settled.

observations.managementAction: The management resubmitted the revised SWM Plan to the EMB for approval.
The management appealed the disallowance to the COA Central Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor a copy of the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year in order for the audit team to verify the existence of the PPE and to ascertain the accounts presented in the financial statements, thus any discrepancies would be immediately communicated to management for its prompt and appropriate action.

We recommended that the Management require the MSWMB complete the approval process of its 10-year SWM Plan in compliance with Section 16 of RA 9003 in order that protection of public health and environment will be ensured.

For the proper management and monitoring of receivables, we recommended that the Management:
a.	Require the Municipal Accountant to send collection/demand letters (at least three times) to the loan borrowers and ensure the same are duly received by the latter. If collection is nil, receiving copies of demand/collection letters will be used to support the request for Authority to write-off dormant receivables.
b.	The Local Chief Executive may file request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts without any possibility of collection in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to present fairly the receivable account in the financial statements.
c.	Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and analyze on a regular basis all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to demand official receipts from the payees/creditors in acknowledging the payment/remittance made by the municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 2004-006 dated September 9, 2004 and in order that the reliability, validity, truthfulness of the payment/remittance made could be ascertained.

We recommended that the Management require the GAD focal person furnish the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report as provided for in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 in order to verify on whether gender issues contained in the GAD Plan were fully addressed.
We recommended that the Management settle their audit suspension before the 90 calendar days period to avoid suspensions maturing into disallowances.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have already reconciled their records.
Management did not charge prior year expenditures from their current appropriation during the year.
Management withheld one percent of the total cost of all procurement of goods as warranty security.
The management have already implemented the ten projects during the year.
The Municipal Accountant withheld taxes from all benefits of officials and employees in accordance with RA 10963 or otherwise known as the TRAIN Act.
The management submitted the GAD Plan and Budget bearing the approval of the DILG Regional Office within the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries/corrections for fair presentation of the account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board complete the approval process of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance with Section 16 of Republic Act 9003. We recommended further that the MSWMB comply with the other provisions of Republic Act 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record expenditures and obligation during the year these are incurred so as to reflect the correct amount of expenses in the financial statements for the year.

We recommended that Management require suppliers to submit sufficient warranty security for delivered goods in the form of either special bank guarantee equivalent to one percent (1%) of the total contract price or  withhold one percent (1%) for every progress payment as retention money and shall duly be released after the lapse of the warranty period.

We recommended that the Municipal Government prioritize the implementation of the unimplemented programs/projects/activities funded under RA 7171 so that the purpose for which the fund was created will immediately benefit the tobacco farmers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure proper withholding of taxes from all taxable personnel benefits of officials and employees in accordance with the provisions of Republic Act 10963 or otherwise known as the TRAIN Act.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GAD Plan and Budget bearing the approval of the DILG Regional Office within the time prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 to facilitate immediate review of the same. We further recommended that the GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding Accomplishment Report be submitted to the Provincial Planning and Development Offices (PPDO) for review in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all officials and employees to cause the settlement of the disallowances and to continue adhering to the documentation of all disbursements to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the effects in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer is in the process of reconciling their records before making the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason:
Inquiry with the Municipal Accountant revealed that reconciliation is still going on.

Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 34 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit his collections daily and intact in compliance with the above mentioned regulation to avoid possible misuse and misapplication of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is facilitating the transfer of their bank accounts to the nearest LBP Branch.
observations.reason: It was noted in the post-audit of collections and deposit that the Municipal Treasurer deposited his collections once a month, usually at the last working day of the month.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and monitoring of the Due from NGOs/POs, Accounts Receivables and Other-Loan Receivables accounts, we recommended the following:

That the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Accountant require concerned employees to intensify collection of the long outstanding receivables and send demand letter, if necessary.

That the Local Chief Executive file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts without any possibility of collection in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Monitor and analyze on a regular basis all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The agency is planning to request for write-off for those debtors which are already dead and those that cannot be located.
observations.reason: Verification from the financial statement showed that the outstanding balance of these accounts amounted to ₱2,658,251.63 as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificates of Title of all parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.
observations.managementAction: The municipality is in the process of securing the required documents needed for the titling of these lots.
observations.reason: Inquiry with the General Services Officer told the team that they are in the process of securing the documents requiring for the titling of untitled lots of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Service Officer to determine all insurable properties of the Municipal Government and to provide an appropriation for the premiums of the insurance of all properties in their Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction: The municipality insured their properties with the GSIS.
observations.reason: Inquiring with the General Services Officer told the team that their properties were insured with the GSIS on October 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile his records with the records of the GSIS and whatever the result would be, the necessary adjusting entries should be made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled with the GSIS and made the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Verification from the financial statements showed a balance of zero (0) of the account Due to GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary documents to facilitate the immediate remittance of the amounts due to the Provincial Government and Barangay Governments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government remitted all amounts due to the Provincial Government and Barangay Governments.
observations.reason: Verification from the financial statements showed that the balance of the account as of December 31, 2019 amounted to ₱292,907.82 which pertains to the last quarter of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following measures/procedures to correct the above deficiencies to ensure the validity of transactions:

That the BAC, thru the BAC Secretariat see to it that, before submitting the contracts for approval to the HOPE, the construction safety and health programs are approved by DOLE.

That the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the Municipality, at all times, be guided with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods and services, infrastructure projects and consulting services.
observations.managementAction: The municipality complied with the audit team’s recommendations.
observations.reason: Contract review of the contracts submitted to the auditor’s offices it was noted that all the required documents with completely attached to the contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality strictly comply with the disbursement procedure for the Special Education Fund. Proper certification by the District Supervisor on Special Education Fund disbursements should be observed.
observations.managementAction: The District Supervisor is already the one certifying the validity, propriety and legality of the SEF disbursements.
observations.reason: Post-audit of DVs for SEF showed that the District Supervisor is the one certifying on the Obligation Request.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should only include programs/projects/activities in their LDRRMFIP those that are in line with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-JCM No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The municipality included in their LDRRMFIP programs/projects/activities in line with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-JCM No. 2013-1.
observations.reason: Post-audit of disbursements pertaining to LDRRMF, it was observed that charges against LDRRMF were all in line with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-JCM No. 2013-1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the BAC Chairman to follow the dates of procurement as planned in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP).
observations.managementAction: The municipality has implemented these projects during 2019.
observations.reason: Review of the Report on Utilization of R.A. 7171 revealed that share from R.A. 7171 received in 2018 were all implemented in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all officials and employees to cause the settlement of the disallowances and to continue adhering to the documentation of all DV’s to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The municipality appealed to the Commission Proper of the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the effects in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit his collections daily and intact in compliance with the above mentioned regulation to avoid possible misuse and misapplication of the funds.

For the proper management and monitoring of the Due from NGOs/POs, Accounts Receivables and Other-Loan Receivables accounts, we recommended the following:
That the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Accountant require concerned employees to intensify collection of the long outstanding receivables and send demand letter, if necessary.
That the Local Chief Executive file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts without any possibility of collection in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Monitor and analyze on a regular basis all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificates of Title of all parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Service Officer to determine all insurable properties of the Municipal Government and to provide an appropriation for the premiums of the insurance of all properties in their Annual Budget.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile his records with the records of the GSIS and whatever the result would be, the necessary adjusting entries should be made.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary documents to facilitate the immediate remittance of the amounts due to the Provincial Government and Barangay Governments.
We recommended that the BAC and/or GSO submit to the Auditor copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommended the following measures/procedures to correct the above deficiencies to ensure the validity of transactions:
That the BAC, thru the BAC Secretariat see to it that, before submitting the contracts for approval to the HOPE, the construction safety and health programs are approved by DOLE.
That the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the Municipality, at all times, be guided with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods and services, infrastructure projects and consulting services.

We recommended that the municipality strictly comply with the disbursement procedure for the Special Education Fund. Proper certification by the District Supervisor on Special Education Fund disbursements should be observed.
We recommended that Management should only include programs/projects/activities in their LDRRMFIP those that are in line with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-JCM No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the BAC Chairman to follow the dates of procurement as planned in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GPB bearing the approval of the PCW within the required time to facilitate immediate review and to identify whether gender issues and concerns of the Municipality were addressed. Likewise attribute GAD related activities/programs equivalent to 5% or more of the total budget of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all officials and employees to cause the settlement of the disallowances and to continue adhering to the documentation of all DV’s to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer return the unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury or to the  source agencies to fully liquidate the accounts in the books of the said agencies and so that they can re-appropriate the funds  for other projects.
We recommended that all Purchase Orders for procurement of goods contain all the necessary information as prescribed by Section B.1 of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure that all shopping and small value procurements of the Municipality comply with the documentary requirements for alternative methods of procurement as prescribed by Annex “H”, Appendix A of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 before payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Government and the Municipal School Board adhere to the programs, projects and activities provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 in the appropriation, allocation and disbursement of Special Education Fund. Also, refrain from charging expenses which are not within the bounds and regulations of SEF so that funds would be utilized exclusively for the purpose/s it was created.

We recommended the following measures/procedures to correct the above deficiencies:

That the MDRRMO prepare the LDRRMFIP and make a separate caption for the special trust fund which are the previous years’ unutilized funds.

That the MDRRMO prepare and submit the Monthly Report on the Utilization of the MDRRMF.

That Management appropriate and implement programs/projects/activities which are in line with those listed under LDRRMC-DBM-DILG-JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and to effect adjustments so as to ascertain the accuracy and correctness of the account Cash-Local Treasury for its fair presentation in the financial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit his collections daily and intact in compliance with Sec. 32 of MNGAS Vol. I to avoid the risk of misuse and misapplication of these funds.

For the proper management and monitoring of the Due from NGOs/POs, Accounts Receivables and Other-Loan Receivables accounts, we recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive  and Municipal Accountant require
Concerned employees to intensify collection of the long outstanding receivables and send demand letter, if necessary.
The Local Chief Executive file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts without any possibility of collection in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Monitor and analyze on a regular basis all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificates of Title of all parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Services Officer to determine all insurable properties of the Municipal Government and that an appropriation for the premiums of the insurance of all properties be included in the Annual Budget and effect the payment of the premiums immediately when due.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile with the GSIS and Provincial Government and to remit reconciled balances of the Due to GSIS and Due to LGUs to the said agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer return the unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury or to the  source agencies to fully liquidate the accounts in the books of the said agencies and so that they can re-appropriate the funds  for other projects.
We recommended that the BAC and/or GSO submit to the Auditor copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within the prescribed period.
We recommended that all Purchase Orders for procurement of goods contain all the necessary information as prescribed by Section B.1 of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended the following measures/procedures to correct the above deficiencies to ensure the validity of transactions:
That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all supporting documents for all types of transactions are complete.
That the BAC, thru the BAC Secretariat see to it that, before submitting the contracts for approval to the HOPE, the construction safety and health programs are approved by DOLE.
That the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the Municipality, at all times, be guided with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods and services, infrastructure projects and consulting services.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure that all shopping and small value procurements of the Municipality comply with the documentary requirements for alternative methods of procurement as prescribed by Annex “H”, Appendix A of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 before payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Government and the Municipal School Board adhere to the programs, projects and activities provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 in the appropriation, allocation and disbursement of Special Education Fund. Also, refrain from charging expenses which are not within the bounds and regulations of SEF so that funds would be utilized exclusively for the purpose/s it was created.

We recommended the following measures/procedures to correct the above deficiencies:
That the MDRRMO prepare the LDRRMFIP and make a separate caption for the special trust fund which are the previous years’ unutilized funds.
That the MDRRMO prepare and submit the Monthly Report on the Utilization of the MDRRMF.
That Management appropriate and implement programs/projects/activities which are in line with those listed under LDRRMC-DBM-DILG-JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Management implement developmental projects which are provided under DILG-DBM JMC # 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund thus achieving desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the Municipality and its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GPB bearing the approval of the PCW and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the required time to facilitate immediate review of the same and to identify gender issues and concerns of the Municipality to be able to attribute GAD related activities/programs equivalent to 5% or more of the total budget of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all officials and employees to cause the settlement of the disallowances and to continue adhering to the documentation of all DV’s to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and to effect adjustments to ascertain the accuracy and correctness of the account Cash-Local Treasury for its fair presentation in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already prepared the reconciliation.
observations.reason: No adjustments in the books were taken up yet.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit daily and intact his collections or as soon as it reaches ₱10,000.00 to safeguard the funds from loss or misapplication.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is remitting his collections weekly.
observations.reason: The Cash-Local Treasury has still a large balance as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the collector at Suyo ECO-Mountain Resort to remit his collections intact and to stop using his collections to pay for marketing expenses. The Officer-in Charge of the resort should prepare the project requirements so as to be included in the Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality and to be purchase in accordance with RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Prompt and intact remittance of collections and inclusion of Suyo Eco-Mt. Resort expenses in the APP.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant complete the Registry of Public Infrastructures from 2002 to 2015 as basis for the computation of the balance of the road networks which should be recognized as PPE in the financial statements in accordance with the aforementioned sections of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015. Also, we have recommended that the General Services Officer, Municipal Engineer and Inventory Committee comply with the duties and responsibilities provided in  the said circular to be able to complete recognition of the account Local Road Networks  in the books at the Municipality in accordance with the  transitory provision of the said circular.
observations.managementAction: Public Infrastructures regarding local road networks from 2002-2015 Registries were already transferred and recognized in the books.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificates of Title of all parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.
observations.managementAction: Secured Transfer Certificates of Title for the parcels of land acquired by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Only four (4) parcels of land which are the Municipal building site and market site were issued Transfer Certificates of Title.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the MDRRMO submit monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on time and the LDRRMFIP be prepared and in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) prepared and submitted to COA.
observations.reason: No monthly Report of Utilization for LDRRMF was submitted to COA and repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment were still charge to the fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the balances of the Due to GSIS and Due to LGUs to the concerned agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Treasurer, Municipal Treasurer and Accountant had a dialogue and reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account.
observations.reason: There was no remittance or adjustments made for the Due to GSIS account and the Due to LGUs has still unremitted amount of ₱292,907.46.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the MSWDO or the concerned employees in charge of the collection of loans issue demand letters if none were sent yet, intensify collection and demand the immediate payment of loans from SULMEA and debtors/employees.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO contacted the debtors/members of the NGO’s/POs.
observations.reason: No collection made however, after the exit conference, Management sent demand letters to the debtors.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a checklist of required documents for each kind of transaction in accordance with COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and attach to DVs so that all concerned officials are reminded and see which document is not yet attach to ensure that all transactions are duly supported with the required documents in compliance with Section 6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the recommendation. The Municipal Accountant was vigilant in checking if supporting documents were complete.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain special accounts under the GF for its public utility and economic enterprises of the Municipality so that Management could monitor their operational efficiency and use the data derived from the financial statements as basis in decision making for the improvement of such.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is already maintaining Special Accounts in the General Fund for the water services of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the GSO and/or BAC submit to the Auditor copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Submitted contracts for infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Not all contracts and POs were submitted to the Auditor within five (5) working days.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the Municipality follow all the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and all other issuances for the procurement of goods and services so that the interest of the Municipality is at all times protected.
observations.managementAction: The BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members followed all provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Some Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed were not posted in PhilGEPs website within the required time and some other deficiencies were noted during post-audit and contract review.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the immediate implementation of funded projects so that benefits derived from them will be timely delivered to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The five (5) projects were already implemented in the 1st quarter of 2017 and no release of RA 7171 fund in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the General Services Officer and the Municipal Assessor look after the processing of the documentary requirements of the acquired lot in order to claim full ownership of the property and the Municipal Accountant submit the said requirements to the Auditor for complete documents of the transaction. Also, for future acquisitions of lots, the documentary requirements provided in Section 13 of  COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012 should be complied in order to prevent any encumbrances resulting thereof.
observations.managementAction: Secured Transfer Certificate of Title No. 2017000771.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all officials and employees to cause the settlement of the disallowances and to continue adhering to the documentation of all DV’s to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Settled all suspensions and disallowances except the disallowed PEI and honoraria of the COMELEC Officer as of December 31, 2017.
observations.reason: The honoraria of the COMELEC Officer was settled on January 18, 2018 under OR No. 7095891.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and to effect adjustments to
ascertain the accuracy and correctness of the account Cash-Local Treasury
for its fair presentation in the financial statement.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit daily
and intact his collections or as soon as it reaches P10,000.00 to safeguard
the funds from loss or misapplication.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the
collector at Suyo ECO-Mountain Resort to remit his collections intact and
to stop using his collections to pay for marketing expenses. The Officer-in
Charge of the resort should prepare the project requirements so as to be
included in the Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality and to be
purchase in accordance with RA 9184.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant complete the
Registry of Public Infrastructures from 2002 to 2015 as basis for the
computation of the balance of the road networks which should be
recognized as PPE in the financial statements in accordance with the
aforementioned sections of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November
25, 2015. Also, we have recommended that the General Services Officer,
Municipal Engineer and Inventory Committee comply with the duties and
responsibilities provided in the said circular to be able to complete
recognition of the account Local Road Networks in the books at the
Municipality in accordance with the transitory provision of the said
circular.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificates of Title of all parcels of
land acquired by the Municipality to serve as proof of absolute ownership
over these properties.

We have recommended that the MDRRMO submit monthly Report
on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on time
and the LDRRMFIP be prepared and in accordance with COA Circular
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the balances of
the Due to GSIS and Due to LGUs to the concerned agencies.

We have recommended that the MSWO or the concerned employees
in charge of the collection of loans issue demand letters if none were sent
yet, intensify collection and demand the immediate payment of loans from
SULMEA and debtors/employees.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a
checklist of required documents for each kind of transaction in accordance
with COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and attach to DVs so that
all concerned officials are reminded and see which document is not yet
attach to ensure that all transactions are duly supported with the required
documents in compliance with Section 6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain
special accounts under the GF for its public utility and economic
enterprises of the Municipality so that Management could monitor their
operational efficiency and use the data derived from the financial
statements as basis in decision making for the improvement of such.

We have recommended that the GSO and/or BAC submit to the
Auditor copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders and their
supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We have recommended that the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG
Members of the Municipality follow all the provisions of the Revised IRR
of RA 9184 and all other issuances for the procurement of goods and
services so that the interest of the Municipality is at all times protected.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
immediate implementation of funded projects so that benefits derived from
them will be timely delivered to the constituents.

We have recommended that the General Services Officer and the
Municipal Assessor look after the processing of the documentary
requirements of the acquired lot in order to claim full ownership of the
property and the Municipal Accountant submit the said requirements to
the Auditor for complete documents of the transaction. Also, for future
acquisitions of lots, the documentary requirements provided in Section 13
of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012 should be complied in
order to prevent any encumbrances resulting thereof.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all officials
and employees to cause the settlement of the disallowances and to continue
adhering to the documentation of all DV’s to avoid suspensions and
disallowances.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit his collections daily and intact in compliance with Sec. 32 of MNGAS Vol. I to avoid the risk of misuse and misapplication of these funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 A dialogue was conducted by the Management and the MTO and he was instructed to remit intact his collections.
observations.reason: Deposits of collections were not made daily and intact. Reiterated in Observation No. 2 page 32 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Engineer to prepare plans/drawings for every infrastructure projects to be bidded and also instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to see to it that the plans/drawings and other documents provided in Section 17.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 are included before issuing the Bidding Documents to prospective bidders to avoid defective infrastructure projects due to incomplete basis or necessary informations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Plans and drawings are already attached to DV’s of infrastructure contract projects.
observations.reason: It was validated during auditorial review that plans and drawing are already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the employees of the municipality not to engage in any business transaction with the municipality whereby money is to be paid or property or any other thing of value is to be taken, directly or indirectly out of the municipality’s resources in compliance with Section 89 a.1 of RA 7160 to protect the interest of the municipality.
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the balances of the Due to GSIS and Due to LGUs to the concerned agencies.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Reconciliation was conducted and LGU is willing to remit the balance of the shares in the books of the Provincial Government.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 7, page 37 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the officials and employees involved in the processing of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to properly fill up the information required so that transactions are completely documented and the validity and propriety of transactions are ascertained.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized DAP fund to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the MSWO and the concerned employees in charge of the collection of loans to exert more effort. Issue demand letters if none were sent yet. If possible, one employee should be designated to personally collect from the debtors.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Personal confirmations by the MSWO was conducted.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 38 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to use Small Value Procurement as alternative method of procurement for repairs and maintenance of equipment, auto parts, and other goods which does not fall under Shopping in compliance with Section 52 and Section 53.9 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted to the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The GSO submitted POs to the Audit Team for inspection of deliveries.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 11, page 42 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the MDRRMC and MDRRMO to appropriate and optimally utilize the MDRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are in line with the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 to ensure that disaster risks are addressed by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 No action taken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 6, page 36 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the concerned officials to implement fully all projects under the 20% Development Fund so as to optimally utilized and targeted beneficiaries could benefit from their timely implementation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Implementation of projects given the utmost attention.
observations.reason: There are only two (2) projects for CY 2016 which was implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Accountant and concerned officials to continue complying with the Provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: There are some DVs of 2016 with lacking documents.
